verdicts.notes: Independent second (craft-skeptic) capture to screens/verify-iter2-craft; all 13 views captured cleanly (no flaky/failed capture). BV9 fix confirmed by LOOKING + pixel measurement: the two hardcoded non-token colors are now var(--text) (18.18:1) and var(--st-success) (5.80:1) on the light view, and they still render correctly in dark. Every in-scope craft BV (BV9-BV13, BV15, BV16) refutation attempt failed to break the artifact; C12 passes. Cross-checks used a pure-Python PNG decoder (PIL unavailable) to distinguish accent (teal) from st-run/st-success and to measure contrast/focus-ring luminance directly from my own captures rather than the maker's PNGs. No regression seen across dark home-live, regressed, crashed, focus, reduced-motion, and not-installed states. Note: criteria C1-C11/C13/C15 and banned B3/B4/B12/B-scope/BV1-BV8/BV14 are outside this lens's assignment (fixture/code lenses); not re-graded here.

I re-ran the capture harness myself (13/13 fresh at 03:45), viewed every PNG + R3, and additionally: 3x-zoomed the two previously-broken light-theme elements, produced a luminance grayscale for BV3/C6, ran mocks/contrast-check.py (exit 0, all tokens both themes), and diffed ui/assets/tokens.css against the design-direction tables (verbatim match). The iter-1 white-on-white active-goal name and pale-green done-label are gone: app.css now uses var(--text) (line 101) and var(--st-success) (line 58); in home-live--light.png 'g2' renders dark-navy #0D1622 and '✓ MAKER' renders dark-green #17752F, both legible on the #FFFFFF surface (worst case is a lighter ground for dark text, so pure-white surface is the best case). Dark home-live matches R3 with no regression. All BV1-BV16 not triggered; B12 satisfied (vision-verify performed with named files); B-scope clean (product code in TARGET only, no engine tests touched, criteria.json unmodified). Verdict: C12 pass; overall PASS. All C-verdicts here are within my VISION+BV9 lens — the non-visual criteria (C1/C2/C3/C5/C7/C8/C9/C15) are covered by their fixture tests, not re-run under this lens.

Host evil.example / evil.example:PORT / 127.0.0.1.evil.com / attacker.test -> 403; 127.0.0.1:PORT / localhost:PORT / [::1] -> 200. pytest -k 'loopback or host_header' passes (subset of 39 passed).

verdicts.notes: Iter-2 change is minimal and complete: 2 hardcoded text colors -> tokens (app.css:58 var(--st-success), app.css:101 var(--text)) + one durable guard test. No over-engineering (guard is a small regex test, not scaffolding); no under-implementation (crash/torn/stale/progress/empty states all still present and probed live). U1 suite: 39 passed (incl. new test_contrast_hardcode_* x2). contrast-check.py exit 0 ALL PASS. Independently verified the hardcode guard is REAL: its regex (?<![-\\w])color\\s*:\\s* flags both #fff and 'white' on a POISONED copy of app.css in a tempdir while ignoring background-color:#000; real app.css untouched, 0 offenders. tokens.css hex diff vs design-direction tables = exact match, both themes. Server exposes only /, /api/state, /healthz, 404 (no static-file route) -> no directory-traversal secret leak. One engine-suite issue: tests/test_p0.py::test_git_precondition ERRORs at setup (fixture 'base' not found) -- PRE-EXISTING and unrelated: no conftest.py exists anywhere in the repo, 'base' is defined nowhere, and the only tracked target change is the additive dash verb; adding a verb cannot remove a fixture. Not a U1-caused regression. All 12/13 iter-1-passing criteria remain passing; the iter-1 BV9 defect is fixed on the code/token side (pixel confirmation deferred to the vision lens).
